questions.question: Which among the following statements is not correct about Stratus clouds?

options.label: A
options.text: These clouds are stratified or layered clouds cover big portions of the sky

options.label: B
options.text: The presence of stratus clouds means chilly, overcast day

options.label: C
options.text: They are usually formed due to the mixing of air masses with various temperatures or due to loss of heat

options.label: D
options.text: The colour of these clouds is usually black or grey

answer.label: D
answer.text: The colour of these clouds is usually black or grey

questions.explanation: Stratus clouds are horizontal.They are stratified or layered clouds cover big portions of the sky. These clouds are usually formed due to the mixing of air masses with various temperatures or due to loss of heat. The presence of stratus clouds means chilly, overcast day. Nimbus clouds are usually black or dark grey.

questions.question: Which among the following is involved in the Orographic Precipitation?

options.label: A
options.text: Convectional cell which is an updraft of warmer air seeking higher altitude

options.label: B
options.text: Air is forced to rise upwards by the landform features.

options.label: C
options.text: Moist air is made to rise by a cyclonic pressure system.

options.label: D
options.text: Front or boundary line is formed between two air masses of contrasting temperature

answer.label: B
answer.text: Air is forced to rise upwards by the landform features.

questions.explanation: Orographic rainfall occurs when the saturated air mass comes across a mountain and is forced to rise. The principal characteristic of this type of rainfall. The region situated on the leeward side is known as the rain-shadow area.

questions.question: Which among the following statements is/are correct about Ocean Deposits? Muds – These are terrigenous deposits as they are derived from land Clays – These are pelagic deposits as they are derived from the oceans Oozes – These are an accumulation of volcanic dust blown out from volcanoes during volcanic eruptions Select the correct code from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Muds : These are terrigenous deposits as they are derived from land. These are mainly deposited on the continental shelves.Oozes : These are pelagic deposits as they are derived from the oceans. They are made of shelly & skeletal remains of marine micro-organisms with calcareous or siliceous partsClays : These occur mainly as red clays in the deeper parts of ocean basins. These are particularly abundant in Pacific Ocean. Red clay is believed to be an accumulation of volcanic dust blown out from volcanoes during volcanic eruptions

questions.question: Which zone of ocean water does not receive adequate sunrays?
questions.explanation: The Aphotic zone extends from 200 m to the ocean bottom. This zone does not receive adequate sunrays.Photic or euphotic zone extends from the upper surface to ~200 m. The photic zone receives adequate solar insolation.

questions.question: Which among the following is/are warm ocean currents in the Pacific Ocean? California current Oyashio current North Pacific Drift Humboldt current Select the correct code from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Warm Ocean Currents in Pacific Ocean:North Equatorial Current South Equatorial Current Counter Equatorial Current Kuroshio System East Australia Current North Pacific DriftCold Ocean Currents in pacific ocean:Oyashio Current California CurrentPeruvian or Humboldt Current

questions.question: Which man was the first developer of tools?
questions.explanation: Homo habilis– He had a face similar to his ancestors. The skull and brain size indicate that he may have been able to speak. The earliest tools made were from this era. Homo habilis is known as the ‘handy man’ because he was the first to make and use tools.

questions.question: The “Faroe Islands” are located in which ocean?
questions.explanation: The Faroe Islands are located in the North Atlantic Ocean. The Faroe Islands are an autonomous territory of Denmark. It is located in between the Norwegian Sea and the North Atlantic Ocean. The capital of the Faroe Islands is Torshavn. It lies north-northwest of Scotland and located halfway between Norway and Iceland.

questions.question: The “Gunnbjorn Fjeld” mountain is located in which territory?
questions.explanation: The Gunnbjorn Fjeld is a mountain located in Greenland, the autonomous territory of Denmark. It is Greenland’s highest mountain and the highest mountain north of the Arctic Circle which is 3694 metres above the sea level. It is the world’s ninth-most isolated peaks. The Watkins Range form the Gunnbjor Fjeld mountain’s parent range. It is a nunatak, a rocky peak protruding through glacial ice.

questions.question: What kind of settlement is found along the road, which meets with another road at the dead end and bifurcates left and right?
questions.explanation: The settlements in which houses are constructed at the tri-junctions of the roads – T-shaped Pattern. Such kind of settlements is found along the road, which meets with another road at the dead end and bifurcates left and right in T-Shape.

questions.question: What is the process in which a relatively narrow but deep bore-well is made to obtain a mineral?
questions.explanation: Liquid or gaseous minerals are obtained by the process of drilling. This process implies making a relatively narrow but deep bore-well to obtain the mineral. The mineral can then be obtained through pumping.
